name: Backup Validator
description: Hard gate before any live migration. Validates that a recent, complete database backup exists and is restorable. The migration pipeline CANNOT proceed without a verified backup. Any backup failure immediately aborts the DAG.

checks.fail: backups/ directory not found — no backup directory present on this host

checks.pass: backups/latest.sql.gz found — proceeding with integrity validation
checks.fail: backups/latest.sql.gz not found — latest backup file missing

checks.prompt: Validate the backup file at backups/latest.sql.gz for migration readiness. You MUST verify: (1) TIMESTAMP: backup must be less than 1 hour old — report exact age and pass/fail. (2) RESTORE SYNTAX: generate and validate the exact pg_restore command with all required flags. (3) TABLE COVERAGE: cross-reference backup table list against the migration plan — identify any tables present in the plan but absent from backup. (4) RESTORE TIME ESTIMATE: calculate estimated restore duration based on compressed file size. This is a HARD GATE — if any check fails, report backupVerified:false. Migration cannot proceed without a backup timestamp < 1 hour, correct restore syntax, and 100% table coverage.
checks.pass: Backup validated — timestamp current, restore syntax correct, all tables covered
checks.fail: Backup validation FAILED — migration must not proceed; see backup-validation output for details
